# Korg / Yvaeh Mode

> An advanced, adversarial multi-agent orchestration harness and self-compounding knowledge vault built natively in Rust. Designed to turn a single CLI prompt into an autonomous, secure, and resilient swarm of cooperative/competitive agents.

## What it is

**Korg** is a high-performance multi-agent execution kernel. It coordinates a parallel swarm of specialized agent personas (Captain, Harper, Benjamin, Lucas) communicating over standard input/output using the **Agent Communication Protocol (ACP v1.17)**. 

Unlike conventional single-agent loops, Korg introduces a harsh, adversarial **Evaluator** persona running 5 distinct binary rubrics, combined with a **cryptographically signed transactional memory (.ktrans)** and thread-safe **Blackboard** database. This architecture guarantees physical execution sandboxing, deterministic crash-recovery, and deep semantic evaluation.

After each execution campaign, Korg runs in **Yvaeh Mode**, automatically scanning its knowledge vault (an Obsidian vault) to reconcile factual contradictions via local BERT embeddings and synthesize new synthesis pages with backlinks.

---

## Core Primitives (The Mechanisms)

Korg's architecture is based on three foundational primitives, specified under `wiki/mechanisms/`:

1. **Leader-Broker Isolation & Worktree Routing** ([[wiki/mechanisms/isolation-routing.md|isolation-routing]]): Spawns worker personas as isolated child processes communicating via piped ACP standard I/O envelopes, guaranteeing absolute execution boundaries.
2. **Transactional Memory Serialization** ([[wiki/mechanisms/transactional-memory.md|transactional-memory]]): Decouples memory state persistence from raw code execution via `.ktrans` journals. Signs every event envelope using Ed25519 signatures, enabling robust state rehydration and three-way merging.
3. **Adversarial Evaluator & Dynamic Guardrails** ([[wiki/patterns/Evaluation-Guardrail-Layer.md|Evaluation-Guardrail-Layer]]): Checks worker outputs against negotiated contract criteria using local BERT cosine-similarity vectors, preventing semantic entropy drift.

---

## Codebase Structure

The reference implementation is written in **~6,534 LOC of production Rust** located at `reference-implementations/rust/grok-acp-harness/src/`:

* **[[main.rs]]**: CLI parser supporting `campaign`, `leader`, `worker`, `reconcile`, and `synthesize` subcommands.
* **[[leader.rs]]**: Campaign orchestrator managing worker process lifecycles, contract negotiation, and transactional merge logic.
* **[[tui.rs]]**: Premium Crossterm/Ratatui split-screen cockpit detailing gauges, active locks, sparklines, and manual overrides.
* **[[skills.rs]]**: Yvaeh Mode workflow orchestrator handling vault indexing, frontmatter parsing, and conflict detection.
* **[[tools.rs]]**: hard-sandboxed tools (`FileRead`, `ShellExec`, `PatchApply`, `TestRun`) with outward shift-resilient patch application.
* **[[evaluator.rs]]**: Evaluator persona checking 5 rubrics (Trajectory, Epistemic, Churn, Resource, Authority) and dynamic semantic entropy.
* **[[acp.rs]]**: Protocol engine handling JCS canonicalization, cryptographically signed envelopes, and standard error taxonomies.
* **[[harness.rs]]**: Standard worker process loop exchanging ACP envelopes with the orchestrator.
* **[[embeddings.rs]]**: Trait-based local embedding engine, utilizing candle-transformers and BERT (`all-MiniLM-L6-v2`) weights.
* **[[personas.rs]]**: Persona configuration mapping strategies for Captain, Harper, Benjamin, and Lucas.
* **[[blackboard.rs]]**: Thread-safe CRDT telemetry ring-buffer tracking active locks, latencies, and transaction logs.

---

## Strategic Roadmap: Surpassing Grok Build, Codex CLI, & Claude Code

A detailed audit and roadmap to surpass commercial platforms is documented in [[wiki/concepts/Korg-Audit-and-Competitive-Roadmap.md|Korg-Audit-and-Competitive-Roadmap]]. 

### Phase 1: Make It Real (Existential Integration)
* **Model-Agnostic LLM Provider Layer (`src/llm.rs`)**: Construct a unified provider client trait supporting OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, Google, and Ollama APIs, replacing static mock payloads.
* **Git Worktree Physical Sandboxing**: Implement real `git worktree add/remove` executions inside [[harness.rs]] to isolate concurrent file access.
* **Adversarial Arena & Three-Way Semantic Merge**: Feed worker proposals to Evaluator, scoring against the contract, and merge the top-N outputs using the LLM.

### Phase 2: Security & DX Superiority
* **LSP Integration**: Leverage `tower-lsp` client inside workers to parse code semantics before applying unified patches.
* **Cryptographic Attestation**: Hash `.ktrans` journals into a Merkle tree and sign the root, emitting secure provenance certificates.
* **Unified Config (`korg.toml` & `KORG.md`)**: Replace hardcoded `/tmp` paths and support project-level instruction sheets (like `CLAUDE.md`).

### Phase 3: Distributed Swarms & Verification
* **Distributed Swarm Orchestration**: Support secure TCP/WebSockets inside [[acp.rs]] to coordinate workers running on separate machines.
* **Formal Plan Verification**: Implement a lightweight DSL compiler to verify Captain's proposed plan DAGs against resource and safety boundaries before execution.
* **Federated Vaults**: Scale Yvaeh Mode to synchronize semantic knowledge across remote developer nodes conflict-free.

---
